digest.en: The US Supreme Court will hear cases on the protected status of Haitians and Syrians. Indonesia is putting four soldiers on trial for an acid attack on an activist. Meta is also facing criticism from the EU for allegedly failing to keep underage users off Facebook and Instagram.
digest.hi:
अमेरिका के खिलाफ संयुक्त राष्ट्र पहुंचा ईरान ने कहा कि उसका जहाज जब्त करना समुद्री डकैती है। ईरान के इस आरोप के बीच, कतर ने अमेरिका-ईरान वार्ता ठप होने पर दो टूक कहा कि होर्मुज को सौदेबाजी का हथियार नहीं बनाना चाहिए। 
अमेरिकी रक्षा मंत्री ईरान युद्ध और रक्षा बजट पर संसद में तीखे सवालों का सामना करेंगे। 
मेक्सिको की राष्ट्रपति ने दावा किया कि अमेरिका ने उनके देश के कानून का सम्मान करने का आश्वासन दिया है।

articles.title: US supreme court to hear whether protected status of Haitians and Syrians can be revoked
articles.summary: The US supreme court will hear oral arguments over whether the Trump administration can revoke temporary protected status for hundreds of thousands of Syrians and Haitians.

articles.title: Indonesia puts four soldiers on trial over acid attack on activist
articles.summary: Indonesia put four soldiers on trial for an acid attack on activist Andrie Yunus. The accused face up to 12 years in prison for the attack that burned one-fifth of activist Andrie Yunus's body.

articles.title: Five takeaways from the King's historic address to Congress
articles.summary: The King addressed Congress with lines that may have supported Democrats. There were some lines in the speech that may have buoyed Democrats – and raised eyebrows in the White House.

articles.title: EU says Meta is failing to keep underage users off Facebook and Instagram
articles.summary: The European Union accused Meta of failing to stop underage users from accessing Facebook and Instagram. The European Union accused Meta on Wednesday of failing to stop underage users from accessing Facebook and Instagram, in violation of the bloc's digital rules that require sites to protect minors.

articles.title: Atharva Vyas, Indian accused of assaulting woman and child detained, ICE called him ‘illegal alien’
articles.summary: Atharva Vyas, an Indian man accused of assault, was detained and labeled an "illegal alien" by ICE. Atharva Vyas, Indian accused of assaulting woman and child detained, ICE called him ‘illegal alien’.

articles.title: Palestinian refugee uses art to share the story of his childhood with his daughter
articles.summary: Basel Zaraa, a Palestinian refugee, shared his childhood story with his 5-year-old daughter through art. He used this medium to explain his past to her. Basel Zaraa was a Palestinian refugee.

articles.title: How the U.K. is reacting to King Charles' visit to the U.S.
articles.summary: King Charles' visit to the U. S. has sparked reactions in the U. K. Lord Peter Ricketts, a former British diplomat, discussed the visit with NPR's Steve Inskeep. during a tumultuous time.

articles.title: Will UAE's departure from OPEC affect the oil cartel's future influence?
articles.summary: The UAE's decision to leave OPEC has raised questions about the oil cartel's future influence. Helima Croft, global head of commodity strategy at RBC Capital Markets, discussed the impacts with NPR's A Martinez.

articles.title: 'Historic homecoming' as endangered antelopes flown to Kenya from Czech Republic zoo
articles.summary: Endangered mountain bongos were flown from a Czech Republic zoo to Kenya in a conservation effort. The species is rare, with less than 100 individuals remaining in the wild. The mountain bongo is a rare antelope endemic to Kenyan highland forests, with less than 100 in the wild.

articles.title: Red card for players covering their mouths in World Cup matches
articles.summary: FIFA introduced a new red card rule for World Cup matches, allowing referees to send off players who cover their mouths or walk off in protest against a decision. New red card rule allows referees to send off players who cover their mouths or walk off the pitch in protest against a decision.

articles.title: Heavy rains threaten Bangladesh's rice harvest
articles.summary: Bangladesh's rice harvest is under threat from heavy rains that have also damaged other crops, including vegetables, raising concerns about potential supply shortages. Heavy rains have also damaged other crops, including vegetables, raising concerns about potential supply shortages.

articles.title: Amadeus to buy French biometrics firm Idemia for 1.2 billion euros
articles.summary: Amadeus agreed to buy French biometrics firm Idemia for 1.2 billion euros, expanding its technology portfolio. Amadeus to buy French biometrics firm Idemia for 1.2 billion euros.

articles.title: Trump On Report Claiming He And King Charles Are Cousins
articles.summary: Donald Trump denied being cousins with King Charles, saying he is "not related" to the British monarch. Earlier, Trump called King Charles "a great guy" and "a fighter". Trump On Report Claiming He And King Charles Are Cousins.

articles.title: Death toll from Indonesia train crash rises to 16
articles.summary: The death toll from a train crash in Indonesia rose to 16 after a long-distance train slammed into a stationary commuter train near Bekasi Timur station. The accident happened when a long-distance train slammed into the last, women-only carriage of a stationary commuter train near the Bekasi Timur station.

articles.title: Ukraine accuses Israel of importing grain 'stolen' by Russia
articles.summary: Ukraine accused Israel of importing grain it claims Russia stole from occupied areas, with Israel disputing the claim that the vessel had entered the port. Ukraine accused Israel of allowing the import of grain it claims Russia stole from occupied areas.

articles.title: Ukraine And Israel Are Fighting Over 'Stolen' Grains. Russia Is The Reason
articles.summary: Israel disputes Ukraine's claim of stolen grain, saying Kyiv provided no evidence. The issue arises as Russia's actions likely impact grain supplies. Israel said that Kyiv had not provided evidence to support its claims.

articles.title: Japan Airlines To Trial Humanoid Robots At Haneda Airport As Labour Shortages Deepen
articles.summary: Japan Airlines will trial humanoid robots at Haneda Airport to address labour shortages. The initiative is a collaboration with GMO Internet Group. The report mentioned that the initiative is being carried out in collaboration with GMO Internet Group.

articles.title: Is India’s Chabahar dream in Iran dead?
articles.summary: India's strategic ambitions for the Chabahar port in Iran are dwindling. The port had been a key part of India's regional plans. The port has been a centrepiece of India's regional strategic ambitions.

articles.title: Calls for humanitarian corridor through strait of Hormuz as Iran war hits vital aid
articles.summary: NGOs call for a humanitarian corridor through the Strait of Hormuz. The Iran war and soaring oil prices are blocking aid deliveries.

articles.title: Opinion | No Peace With 'Peacebroker'? Why Iran Just Bombed A Pak-Based Terror Camp
articles.summary: Iran bombed a Pakistan-based terror camp targeting Jaish-al-Adl. The strikes occurred after similar actions last week in the border regions. Just last week, Iran carried out strikes on terror camps inside Pakistan, targeting Jaish-al-Adl, an organisation that has long been operating in the border regions.

articles.title: EU countries, lawmakers fail to reach deal on watered-down AI rules
articles.summary: EU countries and lawmakers failed to reach a deal on watered-down AI rules. The proposed rules, considered the strictest in the world, aimed to address concerns about AI's impact on children, workers, companies, and cybersecurity. Talks stalled without a new agreement.

articles.title: अमेरिका के खिलाफ संयुक्त राष्ट्र पहुंचा ईरान: कहा- हमारा जहाज जब्त करना समुद्री डकैती; सुरक्षा
articles.summary: ईरान ने अमेरिका के खिलाफ संयुक्त राष्ट्र सुरक्षा परिषद में शिकायत दर्ज कराई है. ईरान का आरोप है कि अमेरिका ने उसके एक जहाज को जब्त किया है, जिसे वह समुद्री डकैती मानता है.

hi.title: अमेरिका के खिलाफ संयुक्त राष्ट्र पहुंचा ईरान: कहा- हमारा जहाज जब्त करना समुद्री डकैती; सुरक्षा

articles.title: किम जोंग उन बेटी को अपनी गद्दी के लिए ऐसे कर रहे हैं तैयार
articles.summary: उत्तर कोरिया के नेता किम जोंग उन अपनी बेटी को अपनी गद्दी के लिए तैयार कर रहे हैं. उनकी बेटी पश्चिमी शैली के कपड़ों में नजर आ रही है, जो उत्तर कोरिया में प्रतिबंधित है.

hi.title: किम जोंग उन बेटी को अपनी गद्दी के लिए ऐसे कर रहे हैं तैयार

articles.title: यूएई का बाहर निकलना ओपेक के लिए कितना बड़ा झटका, दुनिया पर इसका क्या असर होगा?
articles.summary: संयुक्त अरब अमीरात ने ओपेक के उत्पादन कोटा नियमों से मुक्त होकर अपनी तेल उत्पादन क्षमता का बेहतर इस्तेमाल करने का फैसला किया है. इससे ओपेक को बड़ा झटका लग सकता है.

articles.summary: कतर ने अमेरिका और ईरान के बीच ठप हुई वार्ता पर टिप्पणी की है. कतर का कहना है कि होर्मुज जलडमरू को सौदेबाजी के हथियार के रूप में नहीं इस्तेमाल किया जाना चाहिए.

articles.title: ईरान-अमेरिका संघर्ष अभी सुलझा भी नहीं, दूसरे युद्ध की आहट; चीन ने भेजे दो-दो युद्धपोत
articles.summary: ईरान और अमेरिका के बीच संघर्ष अभी सुलझा नहीं है, और एक दूसरे युद्ध की आहट है। चीन ने इस स्थिति पर दो युद्धपोत भेजे हैं। यह विकास क्षेत्र में तनाव को बढ़ा सकता है और वैश्विक सुरक्षा पर इसका प्रभाव पड़ सकता है।

articles.title: यूएई ने छोड़ा ओपेक और ओपेक प्लस का साथ, क्या ये संगठन के 'अंत की शुरुआत' है?
articles.summary: संयुक्त अरब अमीरात ने घोषणा की है कि वह ओपेक और ओपेक प्लस से बाहर हो रहा है. इस फ़ैसले के असर और ओपेक पर संकट के बादलों पर चर्चा हो रही है. यूएई के इस कदम से ओपेक की संरचना पर सवाल उठ रहे हैं.
